chunks.content:
Article: Shinjis Academic Performance
[]: Shinji's Academic Performance             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 30, 2005 10:07 am Bit of an obscure point here, but an interesting one. In the manga, it's implied by Touji that Shinji is in fact towards the top of the class acedemically. However this seems to be contradicted by the anime in episode #10 when it is revealed that Shinji didn't do to well in his exams. Sadamoto might not be the first to assume that because of Shinji's quiet and introverted nature he might be more academically inclined or apt. I had assummed from the episode #10 , his general lack of confidence, and from his emotionally deprived childhood, that Shinji was possibly an average or below average student. However, he could have just let his grades slip under work related pressure. Just on this Touji and Kensuke on occassion refer to Shinji as "sensei". Now I had assummed the two were being facetious, using "sensei" in the same way english speakers might use "buddy", "pal", "man" or some other throwaway remark. But is this in fact Shinji's nickname or the like? If so, does it refer to his introverted, perhaps meditative, appearance, or does to allude to his academic performance? So, is it likely that Shinji performs at an above average level academically?

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Thu Jun 30, 2005 11:31 am Didnt they only call him Sensei when they thought he was trying to peep on Rei? Thats the only time I can recall offhand. EDIT: Ah I think they also did it when Shinji was complaining about Misato, anyway I think they only call him that when remarking on the way Shinji relates to women.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jun 30, 2005 1:23 pm Well, ep#10 shows that he's not particularly with it so far as basic physics goes; so I'd expect that if he shines, it's going to be in the softer disciplines (the ones usually damned as "stamp collecting"). I guess that rote-learning Kanji may slow down other subjects, but thermal expansion was the sort of thing that would be a year or two earlier in the curriculum if he were

Article: Shinjis Academic Performance
 in the softer disciplines (the ones usually damned as "stamp collecting"). I guess that rote-learning Kanji may slow down other subjects, but thermal expansion was the sort of thing that would be a year or two earlier in the curriculum if he were in a UKan school c. 1970.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Thu Jun 30, 2005 1:50 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I guess that rote-learning Kanji may slow down other subjects, but thermal expansion was the sort of thing that would be a year or two earlier in the curriculum if he were in a UKan school c. 1970.

[]:             Quote Post by Oroboro » Thu Jun 30, 2005 9:57 pm work related pressure

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Thu Jun 30, 2005 10:14 pm my thoughts on this - Well Shinji does suffer from self esteem issues alright. but that doesn't neccessarily effect his learning in school. In school he seems for the most part to pay attention, and in the first episode he is reading that NERV book that Misato gives him. so he must like to read somewhat. and also he is doing schoolwork while Asuka and Rei and swimming in episode 10 magma diver. so he seems to like work like someone already said 'because he is so quiet'. other than that they don't give you enough evidence that Shinji is very smart. but I think someone who pilots an eva that good is smart enough.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Bud » Thu Jun 30, 2005 11:55 pm However a contradiction to the contradiction in episode 10 could be that of when Asuka said, "How can he get A's on tests and not in the real world?". Hell if I know, maybe he took up drugs for a while at school. Caused him to slip for a little bit. (First post. Whoopie.)

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Fri Jul 01, 2005 2:50 am It's my view that the manga can never contradict the anime, nor vice-versa, because they are in alternate universes. The manga cannot inform our reading(1) of the anime, and the anime cannot inform our reading of the manga. That's my view. (1) It's a lit. student thing. You refer to "reading" and "the text" even when speaking of a film.
